input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML ŌĆö use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=Black, Dark=White}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=Black}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The `Light` value applies when the OS is in light mode; `Dark` when in dark mode. Changes are **automatic** ŌĆö no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent ŌĆö use `SetAppTheme` extension method:**

```csharp
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);
//                 Ō¢▓ property              Ō¢▓ light value  Ō¢▓ dark value

var button = new Button { Text = "Click Me" };
button.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
                   Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
                   Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Reacts automatically** | Bound properties update instantly when the user toggles the OS theme |
| **Works on any `BindableProperty`** | Colors, images, font sizes, margins, etc. |
| **Scalable approach** | For many themed values, define colors in a `ResourceDictionary` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` inside the `AppThemeBinding` or use separate light/dark resource dictionaries |

**Tip ŌĆö using resources for cleaner XAML:**

```xml
<!-- In App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg"
           x:DataType="x:String"  />
</Application.Resources>

<!-- Better: define themed colors inline where used, or use styles -->
<Style TargetType="Label">
    <Setter Property="TextColor"
            Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=Black, Dark=White}" />
</Style>
```

This keeps individual pages free of repeated `AppThemeBinding` declarations.
